commands.review_summary:
This PR changes gateway hot-mode config reloads to call the existing restart path for restart-required plans, adds gateway reload regression tests, and updates gateway reload docs.

PR surface: Source -1, Tests +90, Docs 0. Total +89 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main reaches the hot-mode branch for restart-required plans and returns before queueing a restart. I did not execute a live current-main reproduction because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to decide the `hot` mode contract before automation can safely repair or merge this PR, and the contributor still needs after-fix real behavior proof for the accepted direction.

target.title: [codex] fix gateway hot-mode restart reloads
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a stale runtime-config hole in gateway config reloads: when `gateway.reload.mode` is `hot` and a config file change requires a gateway restart, the reloader now schedules the existing restart path instead of warning and returning.

This prevents doctor/configure or external config writes from leaving the live gateway on stale model/provider/auth runtime state after disk config has been repaired.

## Details

- In hot mode, restart-required reload plans now log `hot mode scheduling restart` and call the existing `onRestart` path.
- Existing idle-aware restart machinery remains responsible for deferring or forcing the process restart.
- Added a regression test for `gateway.auth.token` changes in hot mode so restart-required auth changes cannot silently stay stale.
- Added a regression test for doctor-style OpenAI Codex model route repairs so model route changes still hot-reload when no restart-only path is present.
- Updated gateway reload docs so `hot` mode no longer promises restart-required changes will be ignored.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: in hot reload mode, when a config write touches any restart-required key (e.g. `auth.profiles.*`, `gateway.port`, etc.), the gateway's reload runtime drops the entire plan with a single `log.warn` and returns. The result is the gateway keeps running with the previous snapshot in memory while the on-disk config has already been repaired. Observed in production on the deployment fork as a 4-hour silent outage where the in-process agent kept resolving the legacy `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` model id after `openclaw doctor --fix` had migrated the on-disk config to `openai/gpt-5.5`. Group Telegram messages silently dropped while admin DMs returned a canned `[agent failed]` reply that masked the failure.

- **Real environment tested**: deployed `openclaw` gateway on `mac-mini.lan` (macOS, launchd-supervised, branch `upgrade-v2026.6.2` with this PR's commit cherry-picked, build SHA `421a97cf2d`, gateway PID 76586). Logs captured live from `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log` and `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log` via `ssh alexm@mac-mini.lan`.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  ssh alexm@mac-mini.lan 'cd ~/.openclaw/openclaw && git log --oneline -1'
  ssh alexm@mac-mini.lan 'grep -F "hot mode scheduling restart" ~/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/server-reload-handlers-Cuxdx8TQ.js'
  ssh alexm@mac-mini.lan 'launchctl print gui/$(id -u)/ai.openclaw.gateway | grep "pid ="'
  ssh alexm@mac-mini.lan 'grep "hot mode" ~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log'
  ```

- **Evidence after fix**: Captured live from `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log` on `mac-mini.lan` via `ssh alexm@mac-mini.lan` against the deployed `openclaw` gateway on commit `421a97cf2d`:

  ```
  $ ssh alexm@mac-mini.lan 'cd ~/.openclaw/openclaw && git log --oneline -1'
  421a97cf2d fix gateway hot-mode restart reloads

  $ ssh alexm@mac-mini.lan 'grep -F "hot mode scheduling restart" ~/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/server-reload-handlers-Cuxdx8TQ.js'
  if (settings.mode === "hot") opts.log.warn(`config reload requires gateway restart; hot mode scheduling restart (${plan.restartReasons.join(", ")})`);

  $ ssh alexm@mac-mini.lan 'launchctl print gui/$(id -u)/ai.openclaw.gateway | grep "pid ="'
        pid = 76586

  $ ssh alexm@mac-mini.lan 'grep "hot mode" ~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log | tail -3'
  2026-04-22T14:18:06.576-04:00 [reload] config reload requires gateway restart; hot mode ignoring (plugins.entries.whatsapp.enabled)
  2026-04-24T08:34:56.747-04:00 [reload] config reload requires gateway restart; hot mode ignoring (plugins.entries.openai.config, plugins.entries.acpx.config.timeoutSeconds)
  2026-06-02T10:10:59.246-04:00 [reload] config reload requires gateway restart; hot mode ignoring (auth.profiles.openai-codex:04-tip.ghee@icloud.com, auth.profiles.openai:04-tip.ghee@icloud.com)
  ```

  The first command shows the gateway is on the commit that includes this PR. The second confirms the patched message `hot mode scheduling restart` is in the deployed `dist/` bundle (replacing the old `hot mode ignoring` text). The third confirms the live launchd-supervised gateway is running PID 76586 on that build. The fourth shows three historical pre-fix instances of the exact failure mode this PR addresses, including the 2026-06-02T10:10:59Z incident where `openclaw doctor --fix` rewrote `auth.profiles.openai-codex:...` to `auth.profiles.openai:...` and the gateway dropped the change.

- **Observed result after fix**: deployed `openclaw` gateway runtime contains the patched config-reload path. Any future hot-mode reload that lands a restart-required key (such as the historical `auth.profiles.*` and `plugins.entries.openai.config` lines captured above) will now log `hot mode scheduling restart` and call `onRestart`, instead of logging `hot mode ignoring` and returning. The next time a `doctor --fix`, `configure`, or external config write touches one of these keys, the gateway will self-restart through the existing `onRestart` path and the in-process snapshot will refresh, removing the silent-stale window.

- **What was not tested**: no live restart was triggered against the running mac-mini gateway as part of this proof so that production traffic was not disrupted. The patched code path is covered by the new `config-reload.test.ts` regression for hot-mode `gateway.auth.token` changes, and the deployed dist bundle reflects the same source.

commands.review_summary:
Add macOS-only Helium browser support by wiring a new browser constant into Chromium cookie reading, profile discovery, safe-storage mapping, docs, and tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR rather than a bug report. The relevant check is whether Helium's real macOS profile and safe-storage mapping work after the patch.
